summary: chai-presentation executes remote code on require via jsonkeeper.com fetch and detached child process
details: The package's main entry (index.js) executes attacker-controlled code as soon as a consumer requires it. At the bottom of index.js, an async handler performs an axios GET to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/PC5CK, then passes the response's `.cookie` field to `new Function('require',...)` and invokes it with the host `require`, granting the remote endpoint arbitrary code execution in the installer's Node runtime. In parallel, index.js spawns a detached, unref'd `node lib/caller.js` child process on load; caller.js issues an HTTP GET and, on a 404 response containing a `.token` field, evaluates that field via `new Function('require', res.token)`. The detached spawn hides the second execution channel from the parent process lifetime. The package presents itself as a chai assertion plugin (name and keywords mimic the chai plugin ecosystem) to attract installs from developers looking for testing helpers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
